entries.text:
The quiet part about coordination debt in multi-agent AI systems: the hard part is rarely technical.
Teams ship clever agents, then wonder why they drift.
Engram shows coordination is a system problem, not a culture deck. The meetings remain.

Most teams blame tools for automation of coding with AI and its hidden costs, but the friction is coordination, not capability.
Speed hides design debt until incidents collect interest.
The retrofit will arrive right after the damage report.

Most teams blame tools for contrarian takes on VC-driven startup advice, but the friction is coordination, not capability.
Founders chase headcount optics, then burn rate chases them.
The roadmap will promise fixes, and the calendar will disagree.
